teamObjective.explanation: My hand is weak and entirely non-trump; the opening lead should shed the lowest card so I keep my slightly higher cards for later tricks.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 cards and no public actions, so their exact composition is unknown. They may hold higher singles or trumps that could win a low lead, but this is only a possibility, not a known fact.

opponentBelief.summary: Each opponent has 3 unknown cards. Given random dealing, it is likely they hold higher non-trump singles or trumps that can beat any of my cards, so they will probably try to seize the lead.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Lead the 3 of spades to discard the weakest card and preserve the 4-6 for future opportunities. Since all legal actions are low non-trump singles, minimizing the value I commit to the trick best limits my loss of card strength.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Only marginally stronger than the 3 and still likely beaten by opponents; it keeps the 3 in hand without clear benefit.
Sacrifices more of my limited high-card potential while remaining vulnerable to any higher single or trump.
Wastes my strongest card on a lead that opponents can almost certainly beat; keeping the 6 maximizes my chance to win or contribute later.

risks: An opponent may play a higher single or trump and win the lead.
The partner may be unable to assist because no public signals have been exchanged.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By committing the 3 first, I retain the 4-6 to respond more effectively in later tricks and avoid burning my best card for no gain.

notes: No public information beyond the new_game event exists, so all beliefs about partner and opponent hands remain probabilistic.
